title: cerastium arvense var. lanigerum
intro: Cerastium arvense var. lanigerum, also known as woolly mouse-eared chickweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals, providing health benefits such as reducing stress and anxiety, promoting healthy digestion, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, woolly mouse-eared chickweed is often used in teas, infusions, and topical creams to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
